No Poverty.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related medical/public health topics.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (4.3) and professional skills (4.4). Core competence B on 'sustainability issues' directly supports education for sustainable development (4.7).
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)
理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: While 'natural resources' are mentioned, the accounting context is too tenuous to link to sustainable water management or sanitation. Model B's revision to 0.000 is accurate.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course is fundamental to economic growth, covering financial statements for capital markets and supporting financial institutions, directly linking to Targets 8.3 and 8.10.
evidence: Comprehend the role of accounting information and financial statements in the capital market.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers 'plant assets' (infrastructure, 9.1) and financial services for enterprises (9.3). Core competence B on 'sustainability issues' relates to sustainable industrialization (9.4).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Financial statement analysis contributes to the regulation and monitoring of global financial markets (10.5), which can indirectly help reduce inequalities by promoting transparency.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, transport, or cultural heritage.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competence B on 'sustainability issues' directly supports integrating sustainability information into corporate reporting (12.6). Accounting for 'natural resources' also relates to sustainable management (12.2).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the course's accounting focus does not directly address climate change mitigation, adaptation, or policy integration.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: The course covers accounting for 'natural resources,' which is a foundational step for integrating ecosystem and biodiversity values into accounts (15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Financial accounting and statement analysis are crucial for institutional transparency, accountability (16.6), and combating corruption and illicit financial flows (16.4, 16.5).
evidence: Comprehend the role of accounting information and financial statements in the capital market.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Standardized accounting practices contribute to transparent financial systems and macroeconomic stability (17.13), though this is an indirect and not a primary course focus.
evidence: financial statements in the capital market
